tournaments.name: IEM Katowice 2026
tournaments.description_short: First major CS2 event of 2026. Spodek Arena, Katowice, Poland. The traditional CS year-opener and one of the most prestigious non-Major CS events.
tournaments.description_long: IEM Katowice has been the traditional first major CS event of the calendar year since the early CS:GO era. The Spodek Arena setting in Katowice, Poland gives the event one of the most-cited live atmospheres in competitive CS history.

tournaments.name: Champions Chess Tour 2026
tournaments.description_short: Multi-event chess season run by Chess.com across the calendar year. Magnus Carlsen and the world top 10 in Division I.
tournaments.description_long: The Champions Chess Tour is a multi-event chess season run by Chess.com across the calendar year, with Division I featuring Magnus Carlsen, Hikaru Nakamura, Fabiano Caruana, and the rest of the world top 10. Format is a series of online events building to a year-end LAN final.

tournaments.name: Six Invitational 2026
tournaments.description_short: Annual Rainbow Six Siege year-opener and the genre's defining event. February in Sao Paulo, Brazil. $3M+ prize pool.
tournaments.description_long: The Six Invitational is Rainbow Six Siege's annual flagship event and the year-opener for the R6 competitive calendar. The 2026 edition runs in Sao Paulo, Brazil with a $3M+ prize pool.

tournaments.name: CS2 Spring Major 2026
tournaments.description_short: Spring CS Major 2026. The first Major of the calendar year and one of the two flagship CS events of the season. Operator and host city to be confirmed.
tournaments.description_long: CS Majors are the flagship competitive events for Counter-Strike, run by Valve-sanctioned tournament organizers (PGL, BLAST, ESL rotate). The Spring 2026 Major is the year's first Major; operator and host city to be confirmed at the time of authoring.

tournaments.name: VCT Masters Spring 2026
tournaments.description_short: Mid-season Valorant tentpole between regional VCT splits and VCT Champions. April or May, host city TBC.
tournaments.description_long: VCT Masters is the mid-season Valorant international tentpole, slotting between the regional VCT split playoffs and the year-end VCT Champions. The Spring 2026 edition runs in April or May.

tournaments.name: ESL Pro League Season 22
tournaments.description_short: ESL Pro League Season 22, the spring half of the year's main CS league competition. Stockholm studio.
tournaments.description_long: ESL Pro League is the year-round CS league competition that captures the in-between-Majors competitive structure for tier-1 Counter-Strike. Season 22 runs in the spring window with the standard round-robin group stage plus single-elimination playoffs at the ESL studio.

tournaments.name: Esports World Cup 2026
tournaments.description_short: Multi-game cross-disciplinary tournament with $60M+ aggregate prize pool. Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. Saudi-funded; politically contentious.
tournaments.description_long: The Esports World Cup is a multi-game cross-disciplinary tournament held annually in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. The aggregate prize pool of $60M+ across all titles makes it the largest single esports event by total prize money. Funded by the Saudi Public Investment Fund.

tournaments.name: EVO 2026
tournaments.description_short: Annual fighting-game championship across Street Fighter 6, Tekken 8, Guilty Gear Strive, Mortal Kombat 1, and rotating titles. Las Vegas, August.
tournaments.description_long: EVO is the annual fighting-game championship and the calendar event that holds the entire FGC together. The 2026 edition runs at the Las Vegas Convention Center across the standard multi-title format covering the load-bearing modern fighting games.

tournaments.name: RLCS 2026 World Championship
tournaments.description_short: Annual Rocket League year-end championship. Tier 1, international, August. The genre-defining Rocket League event.
tournaments.description_long: The Rocket League Championship Series World Championship is the annual year-end event for competitive Rocket League. Run by Psyonix and Epic Games, RLCS Worlds is the calendar tentpole that closes the competitive season.

tournaments.name: The International 2026
tournaments.description_short: Dota 2's annual flagship event. October 2026, host city to be confirmed. The defining event of the Dota 2 competitive year.
tournaments.description_long: The International is Dota 2's annual flagship competitive event and the defining tournament of the Dota 2 calendar year. Run by Valve since 2011, even with the prize-pool decline post-Battle-Pass discontinuation, TI remains the genre-defining Dota 2 event.

tournaments.name: League of Legends World Championship 2026
tournaments.description_short: League of Legends Worlds 2026. October to November tentpole. Single most-watched esports event of the year by viewership.
tournaments.description_long: The League of Legends World Championship is the annual tentpole event for competitive LoL and the single most-watched esports event of the year by viewership. Worlds 2026 runs October to November.

tournaments.name: VCT Champions 2026
tournaments.description_short: Valorant year-end championship across VCT Americas, EMEA, Pacific, and China. The Valorant calendar's defining event.
tournaments.description_long: VCT Champions is the year-end Valorant championship across the four regional VCT leagues (Americas, EMEA, Pacific, China). Run by Riot Games since 2021, Champions is the calendar's defining Valorant event.

tournaments.name: ALGS 2026 Championship
tournaments.description_short: Apex Legends Global Series year-end finals. The defining BR esports calendar event.
tournaments.description_long: The ALGS Championship is the year-end finals of the Apex Legends Global Series. ALGS is one of the few BR esports formats that reads on a calendar (Fortnite is event-bursty by comparison) and the Championship closes the competitive season.
